description: A designed ICE 'rogues-gallery' graphic on a black background with a faint dark topographic-contour texture. At the top, a small red double-chevron icon precedes the white bold word 'MEXICO', labeling the subject's country of origin. Centered is a framed booking-style mugshot photo of a middle-aged man with graying hair, a mustache and a short goatee, photographed against a gray jail wall. Across the lower portion of the photo is a bold bright-red banner with a slight drop-shadow tab, containing the single white all-caps word 'MURDER' identifying the alleged/convicted offense. At the bottom is the official DHS/ICE seal next to the white 'ICE' wordmark, followed by an italic 'in' and the location 'DOVER DELAWARE'. The visual style mimics a sports-broadcast or 'WANTED'/'BUSTED' trading card: high-contrast dark palette, condensed sans-serif typography, and a single shocking red crime label. Per the tweet, the man is Rodrigo Mauricio Lopez-Garcia, a Mexican national sentenced to 60 years (suspended to 15) for 2nd-degree murder and weapon possession after re-entering the U.S. following prior removals. The propaganda technique pairs an unflattering mugshot with a one-word violent-crime label to associate immigration with violent crime and to portray ICE as protecting communities; the target audience is the general public and the agency's social-media followers.
notable_text: MEXICO; MURDER; ICE in DOVER DELAWARE
